pi_items.source: Task specification confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 12, 16) and export_result.sh (line 8)

Calculated from task specifications: Outer box (100×60×30) - interior cavity (96×56×28) + standoffs (4× π×3²×5) - holes (4× π×1.25²×7) - USB cutout (12×12×2) = 29,472 + 565.49 - 137.44 - 288 = 29,612.04 mm³
Specified in task.json metadata. Referenced in verifier.py line 76-78 as tolerance threshold. Reasonable tolerance (±1,480.6 mm³) for manufacturing specs.
Task description states 'External Dimensions: 100mm (Length X)'. Referenced in verifier.py line 60 expected dimensions.
Task description states '60mm (Width Y)'. Referenced in verifier.py line 60 expected dimensions.
Task description states '30mm (Height Z)'. Referenced in verifier.py line 60 expected dimensions.
Referenced in verifier.py line 64 as tolerance threshold. 1mm tolerance is standard for dimensional verification in CAD tasks.

privileged_info_summary: The task is to design a PCB housing box with external dimensions 100mm×60mm×30mm, uniform 2mm wall/floor thickness, and an open top. The housing includes 4 internal cylindrical standoffs (6mm diameter, 5mm height, with 2.5mm through-holes) and a 12mm×12mm USB port cutout on the right wall. The calculated target volume is 29,612 mm³ (tolerance ±1,480.6 mm³, or ±5%). The expected output is a FreeCAD file saved to /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/pcb_housing.FCStd. All bounding box dimensions (100×60×30mm) and tolerances (1mm) are verified against the specifications.
